title: rosa glutinosa subsp. dalmatica
intro: Rosa glutinosa subsp. dalmatica, also known as the Dalmatian rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa glutinosa plant, which is a type of wild rose native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rosa glutinosa subsp. dalmatica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for stress relief and menstrual issues. The plant is often prepared as an herbal tea or infused oil, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
